title: Aluminum Price Analysis Today - Apr 3, 2026
summary: Aluminum futures retreated by 1.62%, settling at $3,461.28 per ton globally following recent massive price spikes. The pullback reflects selling pressure as markets priced in an anticipated influx of imported units to rebuild depleted inventories, alongside easing procurement anxieties following newly announced U.S. tariff reductions on specific aluminum derivative goods.

title: Aluminum fell to 3,468.50 USD/T on April 2, 2026
summary: Aluminum futures in the UK slipped toward $3,500 per tonne but remained near their highest level in over four years, amid fears of a supply squeeze following geopolitical tensions. President Trump warned of severe military action against Iran over the next two to three weeks, contributing to market uncertainty about future supply disruptions.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel rebar futures fell below CNY 3,100 per ton, reaching a three-week low due to demand concerns and policy uncertainty. Prices faced pressure from reports of the Trump administration revising tariffs on steel and aluminum, keeping some near 50% while lowering others to 25%. Steel rose slightly to 3,105 CNY/T on April 2, up 0.06% from the previous day.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel rose to 1,063.94 USD/T on March 31, up 0.09% from the previous day, marking the highest since January 2024. Over the past month, prices increased 4.72% and are up 18.88% year-over-year. In recent weeks, Hot-Rolled Coil Steel gained up to 9.15%.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent disruptions in production.

title: Copper dips as oil rallies after Trump flags more Iran attacks
summary: Copper prices experienced a dip as oil markets rallied following statements about potential Iran attacks. The article reflects ongoing market volatility driven by geopolitical tensions affecting commodity prices.

title: Copper: Chile output slump and mine cuts – ING
summary: Chile's copper output is experiencing a slump with mine production cuts being implemented. This development impacts global copper supply dynamics as Chile is a major copper producer.

title: Ipsa Chile up 2.1% on Iran rally, Copper low
summary: Chilean equity markets rose 2.1% amid Iran-related geopolitical developments, while copper prices remained low. The article highlights the correlation between geopolitical events and commodity market movements.

title: Barrick warns of 'significant increases' to budget, timeline for Pakistan copper project
summary: Barrick Gold has warned of substantial budget and timeline increases for its Reko Diq copper project in Pakistan's Balochistan province, one of the world's largest undeveloped copper deposits.

title: Chinese copper miners join $1.2 billion African rail revamp
summary: Copper producers CMOC and Zijin are partnering with state-owned CCECC to upgrade a 1,860-km rail line to Dar es Salaam in Tanzania, representing a $1.2 billion investment in African mining infrastructure.

title: KGHM mine in Chile reaches early contract agreement with unions
summary: KGHM's Chilean mining operations reached early labor contract agreements covering more than 1,600 employees across multiple unions, securing labor stability for copper production.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology has achieved a breakthrough by producing copper at industrial scale in just 18 months, compared to the typical 18-year development timeline. The technology demonstrates up to 80% less water usage and up to 60% lower carbon emissions than conventional copper processing, with Johnson Camp copper achieving a mine-to-metal carbon footprint of 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper—the lowest in the U.S.

title: Sustainable and efficient operations for copper miners
summary: Copper miners are increasingly adopting sustainable and efficient operations through digitalization and new technologies to meet growing demand from clean energy and address supply deficits. Companies are launching low-carbon copper products, with examples like Boliden's low-carbon copper defined as metal produced with less than 1.5 kg CO₂/kg Cu, approximately 38% less than the global average.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: The copper industry is focusing on recycling, resource efficiency, and technological innovation to support the energy transition, with copper demand for clean energy projected to nearly double by 2050. Nearly 80% of all copper ever mined remains in use today, and approximately one-third of global copper demand is met through recycling, minimizing the need for new mining.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: The copper market faces a projected 10 million tonne shortfall by 2035 as demand grows from 26 Mt annually in 2024 to around 35 Mt in 2035. Technology developments are focusing on exploiting primary sulfide ores, improving mining efficiency by reducing energy requirements, and reducing environmental impacts through decreased water usage and habitat destruction.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: Over 90% of new electric generating capacity installed in 2025 worldwide was solar and wind, driving substantial copper demand growth. AI and data centers represent a key new demand vector, with Asia expected to account for 60% of incremental copper demand through 2040.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Global nickel prices retreated 0.93% to $17,081.3 per ton, with Chinese markets slipping to ¥117,648 per ton. This downward movement is primarily driven by persistent structural oversupply and sluggish downstream demand from the Asian stainless steel sector. Elevated LME inventories, rising Chinese social stocks, and a robust US dollar further pressure prices, though Indonesia’s mining quota restrictions provide a cost floor.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures are trading around $17,200, falling from March highs near $17,770 amid structural tightness and cautious investor sentiment. Nickel fell to 17,028.75 USD/T on April 2, 2026, down 1.48% from the previous day. Supply constraints from Indonesia's proposed export tax and Strait of Hormuz disruptions add pressure.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: The world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, PT Weda Bay Nickel, has been notified to cut its ore production quota to 12 million tons this year from 42 million tons in 2025, as the country aims to boost global prices of nickel. The company intends to apply for a revision of the quota. Nickel prices climbed last month following Indonesia's plans to reduce mine output.

title: Nickel Price Today: Indonesia Supply Cuts Drive Market Near 19-Month Highs
summary: Indonesia has cut its 2026 nickel ore production quotas by about 34% to 250-260 million tonnes compared to last year, aligning output with domestic processing capacity and conserving reserves. This supply tightening, combined with recovering demand from Chinese stainless steel and EV battery sectors, has driven nickel prices to $18,639 USD near 19-month highs. The market anticipates continued support from this disciplined supply approach.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Indonesia set flat-to-down 2026 nickel ore production targets at 250-260 million tons, supporting nickel prices toward $20,000 per ton amid robust EV demand growth of 20% globally. Analysts emphasize the need for confirmed production discipline from Indonesia, with demand forecasted at 6-7% annual growth driven by EV expansion. Canada Nickel's projects position it to benefit from emerging supply deficits.

title: How Nusa Nickel Corp Has Successfully Gained Access to Indonesia's Nickel Market
summary: Nusa Nickel Corp has established itself as North America's only revenue-generating nickel operation and trader in Indonesia, which dominates 60-65% of global nickel supply. Indonesia currently operates about 65 active nickel smelters and 12 HPAL processors, highlighting its extensive processing capacity. The company's leadership has positioned it within this key nickel ecosystem.
